title: 雨天车厢里的水渍
coverQuote: Everyone on the rainy day train is wet in a different place. Left shoulder. Right knee. Backpack. Hair.

sentences.en: The train on a rainy day smells different. Wet wool. Wet nylon. Wet newspaper.
sentences.zh: 雨天的地铁闻起来不一样。湿羊毛。湿尼龙。湿报纸。

sentences.en: Everyone on the rainy day train is wet in a different place. Left shoulder. Right knee. Backpack. Hair. No one is fully dry.
sentences.zh: 雨天车厢里每个人湿的地方都不一样。左肩。右膝。背包。头发。没有一个人是完全干的。

sentences.en: The floor of the car is tracked with muddy footprints. They crisscross in all directions, a map of everyone's morning.
sentences.zh: 车厢地板上踩满了泥脚印。它们交错纵横，是每个人早晨的地图。

sentences.en: A woman next to me wipes her glasses with a tissue. They fog up again immediately.
sentences.zh: 我旁边的女人用纸巾擦眼镜。马上又起雾了。

sentences.en: I hold the rail and feel someone else's wet sleeve brush against my hand. Neither of us apologizes. It is a rainy day. These things happen.
sentences.zh: 我扶着栏杆，别人的湿袖子擦过我的手。我们都没道歉。下雨天。这些事就是会发生的。

title: 撑着伞走路回家
coverQuote: Walking home under an umbrella, the world narrows to a small dry circle. Everything outside the circle is suggestion.

sentences.en: I decide to walk home instead of taking the bus. I have an umbrella and no reason to rush.
sentences.zh: 我决定走回家，不坐公交了。我有伞，也没有赶路的理由。

sentences.en: Walking home under an umbrella, the world narrows to a small dry circle. The circle moves with me. Everything outside is suggestion.
sentences.zh: 撑着伞走路回家，世界缩小成一个小小的干燥圆圈。这个圈随着我移动。圈外的一切都是暗示。

sentences.en: Headlights reflect off the wet asphalt in long yellow streaks. I step over them like puddles.
sentences.zh: 车灯在湿沥青上反射出长长的黄色光带。我像跨水坑一样跨过它们。

sentences.en: A couple passes me, sharing one umbrella. The taller one holds it. The shorter one holds the taller one's arm. Neither of them is dry.
sentences.zh: 一对情侣从我身边经过，共撑一把伞。高个子的撑伞。矮个子的挽着高个子的胳膊。两个人都没怎么遮到。

sentences.en: I take the long way. Past the closed flower shop. Past the 24-hour pharmacy. The umbrella makes a soft drumming sound the whole way.
sentences.zh: 我绕了远路。经过了打烊的花店。经过了24小时药店。一路上雨伞都在发出轻轻的鼓点声。

sentences.en: By the time I get home, my shoes are soaked but my head is dry. I count that as a win.
sentences.zh: 到家的时候鞋子湿透了，但头是干的。我把这算作一次胜利。

title: 书店门口避雨
coverQuote: I ducked into a bookstore to escape the rain and ended up staying for an hour. These things happen.

sentences.en: The rain caught me two blocks from home. I ducked into the nearest open door. It was a bookstore.
sentences.zh: 雨在离家两个街区的地方抓住了我。我钻进了最近的一扇开着的门。是一家书店。

sentences.en: The owner looked up from her newspaper and said: you can leave your wet umbrella in the bucket by the door.
sentences.zh: 店主从报纸上抬起头说：湿伞可以放在门边的桶里。

sentences.en: I did not plan to stay. But the rain got heavier. And the bookstore had a chair in the back corner.
sentences.zh: 我没打算待的。但雨越下越大。而且书店后角有一把椅子。

sentences.en: I picked up a book about birds. Not because I like birds. Because the cover was blue and the rain was gray.
sentences.zh: 我拿起一本关于鸟的书。不是因为喜欢鸟。是因为封面是蓝色的，而雨是灰色的。

sentences.en: An hour passed. The rain stopped. I bought the bird book. The owner wrapped it in a paper bag so it would not get wet.
sentences.zh: 一个小时过去了。雨停了。我买了那本鸟书。店主用纸袋包好，这样就不会湿。

title: 雨刷器
coverQuote: Windshield wipers are the metronome of a rainy drive. Back and forth. Back and forth. Keeping time with the rain.

sentences.en: I am in the passenger seat of my friend's car. The rain is steady now, not too heavy, not too light.
sentences.zh: 我坐在朋友车的副驾驶座上。雨势稳定了，不大不小。

sentences.en: The windshield wipers swing back and forth. Rubber on glass. A rhythmic squeak on the return stroke.
sentences.zh: 雨刷器来回摆动。橡胶刮过玻璃。每次回程都发出一声有节奏的吱嘎。

sentences.en: Windshield wipers are the metronome of a rainy drive. Back and forth. Back and forth. Keeping time with the rain.
sentences.zh: 雨刷器是雨天驾驶的节拍器。来。回。来。回。和雨同步。

sentences.en: The traffic lights ahead blur into red and green smears on the wet windshield. My friend turns down the music to concentrate.
sentences.zh: 前方的红绿灯在湿挡风玻璃上糊成红色和绿色的色块。朋友把音乐关小了，专心开车。

sentences.en: I trace a line on the fogged side window. It drips. I wipe it with my sleeve.
sentences.zh: 我在侧面雾窗上画了一条线。它淌下来了。我用袖子擦掉。

sentences.en: We drive without talking, just the wipers. It is not an awkward quiet. It is a rainy Tuesday quiet.
sentences.zh: 我们除了雨刷声什么也没说。不是尴尬的安静。就是下雨的周二安静。

title: 大雨前
coverQuote: The sky turned that specific shade of yellow-gray that means the rain is about to get serious.

sentences.en: I walked out of the office and looked up. The sky was wrong.
sentences.zh: 我走出办公室抬头一看。天空不对劲。

sentences.en: The sky turned that specific shade of yellow-gray that means the rain is about to get serious. The trees were already swaying.
sentences.zh: 天空变成那种特定的黄灰色，意思是雨要开始认真了。树已经在摇晃了。

sentences.en: People around me started walking faster. A woman ahead of me broke into a jog, holding her handbag over her head preemptively.
sentences.zh: 周围的人开始加快脚步。前面一个女人跑了起来，先把包举在头上。

sentences.en: I felt the first drop on my forearm. Then another on my cheek. Then the sky opened.
sentences.zh: 第一滴雨落在我的前臂上。然后又一滴在脸颊上。然后天开了。

sentences.en: I ducked under the awning of a bank. The rain hit the pavement so hard it bounced back up in a fine mist.
sentences.zh: 我钻到一家银行的雨棚下。雨砸在人行道上，弹回来变成一层细雾。

sentences.en: Five minutes later the street was empty. Just rain and me and a man in a bank doorway who had the same idea.
sentences.zh: 五分钟后街上就空了。只有雨、我和银行门口另一个跟我们想法一样的男的。

title: 午夜的雨
coverQuote: Midnight rain is better than daytime rain. No one expects you to do anything about it.

sentences.en: The rain started after midnight. I was already in bed.
sentences.zh: 雨在午夜之后开始下了。我已经在床上。

sentences.en: Midnight rain is better than daytime rain. No one expects you to go out in it. You can just listen.
sentences.zh: 午夜的雨比白天的雨好。没人指望你在这种天气出门。你只需要听。

sentences.en: I opened my window a little. The sound came in clearer. Rain on the roof. Rain in the gutters. Rain on the leaves of the plant on my windowsill.
sentences.zh: 我把窗户开了一小条缝。雨声更清楚了。打在屋顶上的。落在排水沟里的。滴在窗台植物叶子上的。

sentences.en: I did not check my phone. I did not turn on the light. I just lay there in the dark with the rain.
sentences.zh: 我没看手机。没开灯。就躺在黑暗中和雨一起。

sentences.en: At some point I fell asleep. The rain was still going. It probably continued all night.
sentences.zh: 不知什么时候我睡着了。雨还在下。大概下了一整夜。

title: 雨滴在窗上的比赛
coverQuote: Two raindrops on the window. They race to the bottom. The one on the left wins. I am the only spectator.

sentences.en: I sit by the window. It is raining outside.
sentences.zh: 我坐在窗边。外面在下雨。

sentences.en: Two raindrops on the glass are racing.
sentences.zh: 玻璃上有两颗雨滴在比赛。

sentences.en: The left one moves fast. The right one stops.
sentences.zh: 左边那颗跑得快。右边那颗停住了。

sentences.en: The left drop wins. It reaches the bottom first.
sentences.zh: 左边的赢了。它先到了底下。

sentences.en: I wait for the next race. Two new drops appear.
sentences.zh: 我等着下一场比赛。两颗新的雨滴出现了。

title: 早高峰地铁的沉默
coverQuote: In the morning rush, a packed train car can be completely silent. Fifty people, zero words.

sentences.en: The 8:15 train is packed as always. I squeeze in and find a spot near the door.
sentences.zh: 八点一刻的车照常爆满。我挤进去，在门边找了个地方。

sentences.en: In the morning rush, a packed train car can be completely silent. Fifty people pressed together, zero words spoken.
sentences.zh: 早高峰，一车厢挤满的人可以完全沉默。五十个人挤在一起，零个字。

sentences.en: A man next to me is reading a novel on his phone. The font is tiny. He scrolls with his thumb every twenty seconds.
sentences.zh: 我旁边一个男的正在手机上看小说。字号非常小。他每二十秒用拇指滑一次。

sentences.en: Someone's backpack is digging into my shoulder. I shift slightly. The backpack shifts too.
sentences.zh: 有人的背包正顶着我的肩膀。我微微动了一下。那个背包也跟着动了。

sentences.en: The train announcement crackles: next station. Half the car shuffles toward the doors. The other half spreads out into the new space.
sentences.zh: 广播噼里啪啦响：下一站。半车厢人挪向门口。另一半人散开，占满腾出的空间。

sentences.en: At my stop I push through the crowd and step onto the platform. The silence is replaced by the echo of footsteps.
sentences.zh: 到站了我挤过人群，踏上站台。沉默被脚步声的回音取代。

title: 写字楼大厅的傍晚
coverQuote: Office lobbies at 6pm all smell the same. Floor cleaner and exhaustion.

sentences.en: The elevator doors open onto the ground floor lobby. A stream of people pours out, each one pulling out their phone.
sentences.zh: 电梯门在一楼大厅打开。人流涌出来，每个人都在掏手机。

sentences.en: Office lobbies at 6pm all smell the same. Floor cleaner. Exhaustion. The faint metallic tang of too many elevator rides.
sentences.zh: 傍晚六点的写字楼大厅闻起来都一样。地板清洁剂。疲惫。太多趟电梯留下的淡淡金属味。

sentences.en: The security guard at the front desk is packing up his dinner container. He wipes the desk with a tissue.
sentences.zh: 前台保安正在收拾他的饭盒。他用纸巾擦了擦桌子。

sentences.en: I push through the revolving door. The outside air hits my face. It is the best thing I have felt all day.
sentences.zh: 我推着旋转门走出去。外面的空气打在脸上。这是一整天里我感受到的最好的东西。

sentences.en: I stand on the sidewalk for a minute, deciding which way to go. Home is left. The bar is right. I go left.
sentences.zh: 我在人行道上站了一分钟，决定往哪走。家在左边。酒吧在右边。我往左走了。

title: 十字路口等红灯
coverQuote: At a busy intersection during rush hour, you can learn everything about a city by watching who crosses first.

sentences.en: I stop at the crosswalk. The red hand is blinking. Cars rush past in both directions.
sentences.zh: 我在斑马线前停下来。红灯的小红人在闪。车在两个方向呼啸而过。

sentences.en: Around me, people check their phones. Adjust their bags. Shift their weight from foot to foot.
sentences.zh: 我周围，人们在看手机。调整背包。重心从左脚换到右脚。

sentences.en: A delivery scooter runs the red light. The driver leans into the turn. A taxi honks.
sentences.zh: 一辆外卖电动车闯了红灯。骑手压着弯转过去。一辆出租车按了喇叭。

sentences.en: The light changes. The crowd surges forward. A woman in heels walks fast. An old man takes his time. Everyone moves at their own speed in the same crossing.
sentences.zh: 灯变了。人群涌向前。一个穿高跟鞋的女人走得飞快。一个老人慢悠悠的。同一个路口，每个人都有自己的速度。

sentences.en: I reach the other side and keep walking. Behind me the light changes back to red. The next crowd gathers.
sentences.zh: 我走到对面继续走。身后灯又变红了。下一群人开始聚集。
